questions.question: In 1932, the Australian military was deployed to deal with a specific animal population in what became known as the 'Great War.' Which animal won this conflict?
What is unique about the waste produced by a Wombat?
Why are the Kangaroo and the Emu featured on the Australian Coat of Arms?
options: They are the only animals that cannot walk backward
They were the most delicious animals found by settlers
They are the only animals that survived the Great Ice Age

questions.question: In 1967, Prime Minister Harold Holt disappeared while swimming. How did Australia later commemorate him?
options: They named a mountain after him
They named a swimming center after him
They banned swimming at that specific beach

questions.question: Former PM Bob Hawke once held a world record for which of the following activities?
options: Shearing a sheep in under a minute
Sculling 2.5 pints of beer in 11 seconds
Winning a marathon in work boots

questions.question: If you visited one new Australian beach every day, how long would it take to see them all?
What is the mythical 'Drop Bear' used for in Australian culture?
options: To teach children about forest safety

questions.question: What is 'Fairy Bread,' a staple of Australian children's parties?
options: White bread with butter and sprinkles

questions.question: Australia has the world's largest population of wild camels. Which country do they actually export them to?
